PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Continued To Nov-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich for and with Quinn Ellis and Lawrence Ellis, Jr.
Jennifer Jaynes for and with Joicelyn Lampkin.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 31, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 7, 2019. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and the Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to and the Parties are to return on November 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. If mediation is unsuccessful, the Parties are to meet and confer by November 7th and provide a joint status statement outlining the issues that are left for trial or hearing, how they contemplate completing that and an estimate of how long the trial will be. In response to Ms. Lvovich's request, the Court informed the Parties that it will not set a trial date while mediation is taking place, but it will set a date at the next hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Continued To Aug-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich for and with Quinn Ellis and Lawrence Ellis.
Jennifer Jaynes for Joycelyn Lampkin (Petitioner).

Ms. Jaynes stated that she is close to completing discovery, that the handwriting expert still needs to examine the will and that she feels it is necessary to bifurcate the issue of standing. The Court stated that it is does not feel the need to do that, that it will be up to the trial Judge to determine that, and that now that Mr. Ellis is in the case, it will not bifurcate that issue. Ms. Jaynes stated that she feels it is prejudicial to her Client and that she may have to do a motion for summary judgment. Ms. Lvovich stated that most of the written discovery is completed on both sides, that the depositions of each party were taken on both sides, that her Client was excluded because of the restraining order, that they still need to go to Court on the restraining order and that the handwriting expert still needs to review the will on both sides. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to August 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court anticipates that discovery will be completed, that the parties have met and conferred as to what the trial will look like, that the parties talk about resolution/mediation, and that the parties submit a joint status statement no later than August 13, 2019 that tells the Court where they are, and if there needs to be trial, how long it will be, how many witnesses there will be and how many exhibits there will be.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition To Amend Order Appointing Special Administrator And For Amended Letters Of Special Administration
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-29-2019 Continued To Apr-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich for and with Anthony Quinn Ellis.
Jennifer Jaynes for and with Jocelyn Lampkin.

The Court noted that Ms. Lvovich's petition is not in a position to go forward. Ms. Lvovich stated that discovery and an evidentiary hearing is needed, and that she understands her petition is not procedurally ready to go forward, but they need to resolve the will contest because her petition is requesting appointment of her Client in an intestate probate. Ms. Jaynes stated that she tried reaching out to Ms. Lvovich a couple of times, that mediation would be futile, that there is only one asset which is the home her Client lives in and that they are not willing to sell. Ms. Jaynes also stated that she feels she has met her burden to prove that the will is valid. Ms. Lvovich stated that they are ready to proceed with discovery, obtain the services of a hand writing expert, subpoena documents from the notary, depose and obtain written discovery from Ms. Lampkin, and that she needs 90-days. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to April 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to complete discovery, meet and confer prior to the hearing as to what the factual issues are, who the witnesses will be, what exhibits there will be, and all of the issues that need to be resolved to get this concluded and how long the evidentiary hearing is going to take. The Court would like to see a joint status statement by April 23, 2019. Ms. Jaynes stated that she would like to have the special letters extended.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Jaynes for and with Joycelyn Lampkin (Proposed Executor/Petitioner).

Ms. Jaynes stated that Ms. Lampkin is able to obtain a hard money loan if she gets a co-borrower, that the co-borrower will not go on the deed and that she prepared a new order which includes one sentence at the end/language regarding the above (proposed was provided). The Court stated that it is prepared to extend the existing terms on their special administration and that there should have been an ex parte request. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to January 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Continued To Dec-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Jaynes for and with Jocelyn Lampkin.
Milla Lvovich for and with Quinn Ellis (Objector).
Lawrence Ellis also present.

The Court noted that there have been various filings and denied Ms. Jaynes' request to deny Ms. Lvovich's petition due to late filings. Ms. Jaynes raised the issue of the reverse mortgage and the need for appointment of a special administrator. Ms. Lvovich stated that she has not filed an ex parte petition for special administration, that she saw it on the Court's register of actions, that it was then shortly taken out and that she thinks it was entered by mistake. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to December 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for everyone to see what has been filed and how this is going to proceed. The hearing on Ms. Lvovich's petition on calendar for December 17, 2018 is also continued to December 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported..

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Continued To Nov-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Larry Ellis, appeared in pro per;
Quinn Ellis, appeared in pro per.

Matter called. The Court stated that before it today is a petition for probate of will and letters testamentary which was on calendar this morning as a pre-grant. Larry Ellis and Quinn Ellis stated that they were appearing to object to the petition. The Court continued the matter to November 13, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 to allow Larry Ellis and Quinn Ellis time to file written objections. The objections are to be filed no later than November 6, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosado. (Not Reported)
